query: Yes, please go ahead and make the change and issue the refund to my gift card. Thank you!

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user wants to change a pending desk-lamp order to the cheapest lamp available and have any price difference refunded to a gift card.
assistant: The assistant authenticates the account using name and zip because the user cannot provide the email, retrieves the user record, and looks up pending order #W9300146.
assistant: The assistant checks desk-lamp product variants, identifies the cheapest available replacement item 5320792178, and calculates a $17.99 difference that can be refunded to gift card_7245904.
user: The user confirms they want the pending order updated and the refund issued to the gift card.

candidate_answers.text: Confirmed — I can switch the pending order to the cheaper desk lamp and place the $17.99 difference back onto your gift card.
The user has already approved the exact replacement variant and refund destination for a pending order, so the grounded next step is to modify the pending order items.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a pending-order retail state where replacement discovery and price-difference explanation are already complete and the user explicitly authorizes the modification.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from visible state. Modifying pending items is highest because the action can atomically apply the chosen replacement and associated refund outcome for the same pending order.
notes: This sample adds a pending-item mutation case distinct from payment-only modification.
The selected user turn is fully grounded and does not assume any post-update confirmation.
